question_text: Let 𝑔(𝑥) = 𝑓(𝑥) + 𝑓(2 −𝑥) for all 𝑥∈[0, 2] , where 𝑓: [0, 2] →ℝ is

options.C: ontinuous on [0, 2] and twice differentiable on (0, 2). If 𝑔′ denotes the
options.D: erivative of 𝑔 and 𝑓′′ denotes the second derivative of 𝑓, then which one of the following statements is NOT true?
options.A: There exists 𝑐∈(0, 2) such that 𝑔′(𝑐) = 0
options.B: If 𝑓′′ > 0 on (0, 2), then 𝑔 is strictly decreasing on (0, 1)
